results.title: The Complete AI Models Guide for 2025 | by Ankit Arora (Wanderer Piscean) | Medium
results.content:
## What Actually Happened in 2025 (And Why It Matters):

1. OpenAI surprised everyone with GPT-4.1 series — I was honestly skeptical about the 1 million token claim until I tested it with a 500-page research document. It actually works.

2. GPT-4.5 (Orion) is still in preview, but from what I’ve tested, the creativity boost is real — especially for brainstorming sessions.

3. Anthropic’s Claude 4 launch caught me off guard. The hybrid architecture in Opus 4 handles complex reasoning tasks that previously required multiple model interactions.

4. Google’s Gemini 2.5 rollout in March was a game-changer for anyone dealing with large files. I’ve been using it for PDF analysis and it’s incredibly efficient. [...] ## What I’ve Noticed After Months of Testing:

1. Context windows have gotten ridiculously large — GPT-4.1’s 1M tokens means I can feed it entire codebases or research papers.
2. Multimodal is now table stakes — every premium model handles text, images, and audio, but the quality varies significantly.
3. Real-time information is becoming the killer feature — models without current data feel outdated quickly.
4. Open-source options like Llama 3.2 are getting surprisingly good — perfect if you need complete control over your deployment.
5. The pricing war is real — several providers now offer genuinely useful free tiers.
6. Specialized models often beat generalists in their domains — DeepSeek V3 crushes GPT-4 on certain math problems. [...] 3. Gemini 2.5 Pro — Solid choice, especially if you’re already in the Google ecosystem.

### For Research and Analysis:

1. Perplexity Pro — I can’t overstate how useful real-time information access is for research.

2. Gemini 2.5 Pro — When I have massive PDFs or datasets to analyze.

3. Claude Opus 4 — For deep, thoughtful analysis that requires connecting multiple concepts.

### Creative and Content Work:

1. GPT-4.5 — The creativity boost is real, though it’s still in preview.

2. GPT-4o — Reliable for most creative tasks, plus it handles images and audio.

### Business and Enterprise:

1. GPT-4.1 — When reliability and advanced capabilities are non-negotiable.

2. Microsoft Copilot — If you’re already using Microsoft tools, the integration is seamless.

The models were trained on Meta’s Research SuperCluster (RSC) using NVIDIA H100 GPUs, although the precise cluster size, training data volume, and total training cost have not been publicly confirmed. The release comprised three model tiers designed for distinct deployment scenarios. LLaMA 4 Scout employs 109 billion total parameters with 17 billion active parameters across 16 experts and supports a context window of 10 million tokens, one of the longest context windows offered by any model at the time of release. The 10-million-token context is achieved through a combination of architectural innovations in sparse attention and [...] Anthropic has not publicly disclosed the architecture type, parameter counts, training data volume, hardware configuration, or training cost for any Claude model. The Claude 4 family, introduced from late 2025 through early 2026, represents a substantial advance across all capability dimensions . Claude Opus 4.5, released in November 2025, established new benchmarks for coding, agentic task execution, and computer use, becoming the preferred model for software engineering workflows that require sustained multi-step reasoning across large codebases. Claude Opus 4.6, released on 5 February 2026, extends the frontier further as Anthropic’s most capable model to date. results.title: The Big LLM Architecture Comparison - Ahead of AI - Sebastian Raschka
results.content:
Llama 4 Maverick uses a more classic MoE setup with fewer but larger experts (2 active experts with 8,192 hidden size each) compared to DeepSeek V3 (9 active experts with 2,048 hidden size each). Also, DeepSeek uses MoE layers in each transformer block (except the first 3), whereas Llama 4 alternates MoE and dense modules in every other transformer block.

Given the many small differences between architectures, it is difficult to determine their exact impact on final model performance. The main takeaway, however, is that MoE architectures have seen a significant rise in popularity in 2025.

# 6. Qwen3 [...] The Big LLM Architecture Comparison                                               

SubscribeSign in

# The Big LLM Architecture Comparison

### From DeepSeek V3 to GLM-5: A Look At Modern LLM Architecture Design

Sebastian Raschka, PhD

Last updated: Apr 2, 2026 (added Gemma 4 in section 23)

It has been seven years since the original GPT architecture was developed. At first glance, looking back at GPT-2 (2019) and forward to DeepSeek V3 and Llama 4 (2024-2025), one might be surprised at how structurally similar these models still are. This includes three smaller dense models (3B, 8B, and 14B) under the Ministral 3 name, as well as their new Mistral 3 Large flagship model, which is a 675B parameter MoE (with 41B parameters active). results.title: Model Comparison: GPT, Claude, Gemini, DeepSeek, Llama | TheoremPath
results.content:
Context length matters for tasks that require processing large documents, codebases, or conversation histories. For shorter inputs, a larger context window provides little benefit.

### Open Weights

Open weights enable: fine-tuning for specific domains, running inference on your own hardware (no API dependency), inspecting model internals for research, and quantization for deployment on smaller hardware.

The main open-weight shortlist to check in April 2026 is DeepSeek, Llama 4, Qwen3, Gemma, Mistral-family models, Kimi, and GLM. The main closed-hosted shortlist is GPT-5.x, Claude, and Gemini frontier models. That division is about access and deployment control, not absolute capability. [...] Preview endpoints can be renamed or shut down. Production code should prefer documented stable strings unless the newest preview is required.

Open-weight systems

#### DeepSeek, Llama 4, Qwen3, Gemma, Mistral-family models

Downloadable weights or permissive model cards, depending on family

Architecture

Often disclosed: MoE for DeepSeek, Llama 4 Scout/Maverick, and Qwen3-235B-A22B.

Context

Ranges from 32K native windows to Llama 4 Scout's 10M-token model-card claim.

Best use

Local control, fine-tuning, inspectability, privacy boundaries, or high-volume inference economics.

Open weights are not automatically cheap. Large MoE models can need enormous memory even when active-parameter compute is low.

#### If correctness matters [...] Architecture

Undisclosed for Claude

Context

Opus 4.7 and Sonnet 4.6 list 1M context; Haiku 4.5 lists 200K.

Best use

Coding, long document work, vision, enterprise workflows, and controlled output style.

Mythos Preview is invitation-only for defensive cybersecurity work through Project Glasswing.

Google

#### Gemini 3.1 previews, Gemini 2.5 stable, Gemma 4

Gemini API / Vertex AI for hosted models; Gemma for open weights

Architecture

Partly disclosed across generations; do not infer one Gemini endpoint from another.

Context

Gemini 3.1 and Gemini 2.5 text models list million-token-class context; exact limits depend on endpoint.

Best use

Long-context multimodal analysis, Google product integration, real-time media, and local Gemma deployment.

Hybrid thinking/non-thinking mode in one model.GPT-5-class performance on coding and math benchmarks | MIT / $0.28 / $0.42 | [...] ### Meta LlamaOpen Source LLMs

Meta's Llama 4 family—released April 2025 —marked a decisive architectural leap from the Llama 3 generation. All three models use a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) design and are natively multimodal, trained jointly on text, images, and video across 200+ languages.

The two available models, Scout and Maverick, introduced the largest context window of any open or closed model (Scout's 10M tokens) and benchmark results competitive with GPT-4o and Gemini 2.0 Flash at a fraction of the cost. Llama 4 Behemoth—a 2-trillion-parameter teacher model used to distill Scout and Maverick—has been previewed but is not yet publicly available.

Weights for Scout and Maverick are free to download under the Llama 4 Community License.

results.title: ✨ The Complete Guide to Choosing AI Models in 2025 ...
results.content:
Whether you’re a business leader evaluating AI tools, a content creator exploring automation, a researcher pushing boundaries, or a developer building applications, you face the same challenge: Which AI model should I use?

The answer isn’t simple. GPT-5.2 might excel at creative writing but cost 10x more than alternatives. Claude Opus 4.5 could be perfect for complex coding but overkill for simple tasks. Gemini 2.5 Flash might offer the best balance for your specific needs — or it might not.

This guide cuts through the noise. We’ll explore the major AI models available in 2025, compare their strengths and weaknesses, analyze their costs, and most importantly, help you make informed decisions based on your actual needs.

## ✴️ Understanding the AI Model Landscape [...] Claude Opus 4.5: The Premium Flagship

 Best for: Complex software engineering, agentic workflows, research, sophisticated analysis
 Strengths:  
  - Industry-leading coding performance (80.9% on SWE-bench Verified)  
  - Excellent at multi-step reasoning  
  - Strong safety and alignment  
  - 200K token context window
 Pricing: $5/M input tokens, $25/M output tokens
 Key achievement: Lowest misalignment rate in its class
 When to use: Complex projects where accuracy and reasoning depth are paramount

Claude Sonnet 4.5: The Coding Champion [...] ### Scenario 4: Complex Research & Analysis

Task: 20 deep research projects per month (long context, complex reasoning)  
Monthly volume: ~1M output tokens, ~2M input tokens

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Recommendation: Gemini 2.5 Pro or GPT-5.2 Thinking for balance

### Cost Comparison Summary

Press enter or click to view image in full size

## ✴️ The Smart Selection Strategy: Model Routing

Here’s a pro tip: You don’t have to choose just one model.

The smartest AI users employ a routing strategy, using different models for different tasks.

### The Three-Tier Approach

Tier 1: Fast & Cheap (80% of tasks)

 Use: Gemini 2.5 Flash, Claude Haiku 4.5, or GPT-5 Mini
 For: Simple queries, drafts, routine tasks, high-volume operations

Tier 2: Balanced (15% of tasks)

gpt-oss-120B is OpenAI's first modern open-weights release, shipped August 5, 2025 under Apache 2.0. At 117B total / 5.1B active, it is the smallest MoE in this list by active parameters. AA's per-provider data puts Fireworks throughput at 70 tokens/sec on the standardized 10K-input workload, with the all-provider median around 190 tokens/sec for this model and Cerebras and SambaNova above 600 tok/s; gpt-oss-120B is therefore not the throughput choice on Fireworks, but it is the lowest-cost model in the list, and at $0.15/$0.60 per million tokens it costs roughly 42x less than Claude Opus 4.7 on output, though at roughly half Opus 4.7's AA Coding Index (28.6 vs 52.5) and best suited to function-level work. [...] Q: Can V4-Flash handle 1M context reliably?

Long-context behavior is vendor-claimed; independent long-context benchmarks are still early. The vendor-reported MRCR 1M score is 78.7% and CorpusQA 1M is 60.5%, both below V4-Pro. Workloads that push the 1M window should plan independent evals.

Q: Will V4-Flash land on Fireworks?

Probable but not confirmed at time of publication. V4-Pro landed on Fireworks shortly after its April 24, 2026 release, and Fireworks' day-zero pattern has held for Kimi K2.6 (April 20), DeepSeek V3.x, and other MoE releases. Check fireworks.ai/models for current availability.

## When should you use gpt-oss-120B (high)?

`/v1/responses`

DeepSeek-V4 Pro is the flagship model in the DeepSeek-V4 preview series, an open-weights 1.6T parameter Mixture-of-Experts model with 49B activated parameters and a 1M token context window. It uses a hybrid attention architecture combining Compressed Sparse Attention and Heavily Compressed Attention for long-context efficiency, adds Manifold-Constrained Hyper-Connections, and supports non-thinking, thinking, and Think Max reasoning modes for agentic coding, knowledge work, and long-context tasks.
